data.body:
## Context

When agents are asked to fix bugs via /dso:fix-bug, two failure modes undermine investigation quality. First, the agent sometimes skips the Skill tool entirely and attempts to fix the bug using its own reasoning, bypassing the structured investigation workflow. Second, even when the skill loads correctly, agents skip hypothesis testing (Step 3) and jump directly to implementation — treating the bug description as confirmed evidence rather than a hypothesis to validate. This leads to fixes that address symptoms rather than root causes, wasted effort on wrong hypotheses, and regressions when the actual root cause is missed.

## Success Criteria

1. All investigation prompts (basic, intermediate, advanced, escalated, cluster) require a structured hypothesis block (HYPOTHESIS → TEST → OBSERVED → VERDICT) as a mandatory part of the RESULT schema — an investigation result without at least one hypothesis block is invalid
2. The fix-bug orchestrator validates that the investigation result contains at least one hypothesis block with a VERDICT of confirmed before allowing fix implementation (Step 6) — missing or all-disproved hypotheses trigger escalation, not implementation
3. No code-modifying action (Edit, Write, or fix implementation sub-agent dispatch) occurs before a RED test demonstrates the root cause — the orchestrator enforces RED-before-fix as a hard gate
4. A user-prompt-submit hook detects when the user's message contains /fix-bug or /dso:fix-bug and injects a directive requiring the agent to invoke the Skill tool before any other action
5. The fix-bug SKILL.md preamble includes a hard gate reinforcing that Steps 1-5 (classify, investigate, hypothesis test, approve, RED test) must complete before any code modification

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Two-pronged enforcement: (A) Add a mandatory structured hypothesis template to all investigation sub-agent prompts and validate its presence in the orchestrator before allowing fix implementation. (B) Add a user-prompt-submit hook that detects /fix-bug invocations and injects a directive to use the Skill tool, addressing the skill dispatch reliability problem.

## Supersedes

Bug d22f-da61 (fix-bug skill skips hypothesis testing)
